questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The correct definition of Meissner effect is
options: the phenomenon of perfect paramagnetism in superconductors
the phenomenon of perfect diamagnetism in superconductors
the phenomenon of perfect diamagnetism in semiconductors
the phenomenon of perfect ferromagnetism in metals
questions.solution: Superconductors in the Meissner state exhibit perfect diamagnetism or superdiamagnetism, meaning that the total magnetic field is very close to zero deep inside them.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
options: net magnetic dipole moment per unit volume.
volume of the sample per unit magnetic dipole moment.
ratio of magnetic dipole moment and pole strength.
ratio of pole strength to magnetic dipole moment.
questions.solution: Magnetization is defined as the magnetic moment per unit volume of a material. It represents the extent to which a material is magnetized under the influence of an external magnetic field.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A jet plane is travelling west at $450 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. If the horizontal component of earth's magnetic field is $4 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~T}$ and angle of dip is $30^{\circ}$, then the vertical component is
questions.solution:
Given, $B_{H}=4 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~T}$

Angle of $\operatorname{dip}, \delta=30^{\circ}$

We know that,

$\begin{aligned}

\tan \delta &=\frac{B_{V}}{B_{H}} \\

\Rightarrow \quad B_{V} &=B_{H} \tan \delta \\

&=4 \times 10^{-4} \tan 30^{\circ} \\

&=\frac{4}{\sqrt{3}} \times 10^{-4} \\

&=2.3 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~T}

\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Inside a bar magnet, the magnetic lines of force
options: are from S-pole to $\mathrm{N}$ -pole of the magnet.
depend upon area of cross-section of bar magnet.
are from $\mathrm{N}$ -pole to $\mathrm{S}$ -pole of the magnet.
questions.solution: Inside a bar magnet, the magnetic field lines(A) are not present(B) are parallel to the cross-sectional area of the magnet(C) are in the direction from $\mathrm{N}$-pole to S-pole(D) are in the direction from S-pole to $N$-pole
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The earth is considered as a short magnet with its centre coinciding with the geometric centre of earth. The angle of $\operatorname{dip} \phi$ related to the magnetic latitude $\alpha \lambda$ as
questions.solution:
For a dipole at position $(\mathrm{R}, \mathrm{Q})$
$\mathrm{B}_{\mathrm{R}}=\frac{\mu_{0}}{4 \pi} \cdot \frac{2 \mathrm{M} \cos \theta}{\mathrm{R}^{3}}$ ...(i)
and $\mathrm{B}_{\mathrm{Q}}=\frac{\mu_{0}}{4 \pi} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{M} \sin \theta}{\mathrm{R}^{3}}$ ...(ii)
Also $\tan \phi=\frac{\mathrm{B}_{\mathrm{V}}}{\mathrm{B}_{\mathrm{H}}}=-\frac{\mathrm{B}_{\mathrm{R}}}{\mathrm{B}_{\mathrm{Q}}}$ ...(iii)


Dividing eq. (i) by (ii)
$\frac{\mathrm{B}_{\mathrm{R}}}{\mathrm{B}_{\mathrm{Q}}}=\frac{2 \cos \theta}{\sin \theta}=2 \cot \theta$
From eq. (iii) and (iv)
$\tan \phi=-2 \cot \theta$
From figure, $\theta=90^{\circ}+\lambda$
$\therefore \quad \tan \phi=-2 \cot (90+\lambda)$
$\tan \phi=2 \tan \lambda$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Which of the following statement related to hysteresis loop is incorrect?
options: The curve of $\mathrm{B}$ against $\mathrm{H}$ for $\mathrm{a}$ ferromagnetic material is called hysteresis loop
The area of $B$ - $H$ curve is a measure of power dissipated per cycle per unit area of the specimen
Coercitivity is a measure of the magnetic field required to destroy the residual magnetism of ferromagnetic material
The retentivity of a specimen is the measure of magnetic field remaining in the specimen when the magnetising field is removed
questions.solution: The hysteresis loop i.e. area of B-H curve is a measure of energy dissipated per cycle per unit volume of the specimen. It depends on the nature of magnetic material.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A magnetic needle lying parallel to the magnetic field requires $W$ units of work to turn it through an angle $45^{\circ} .$ The torque required to maintain the needle in this position will be
questions.solution:
Work done by magnet to turn from angle
$\begin{array}{l}
\begin{array}{l}
\theta_{1} \text { to } \theta_{2} \\
\mathrm{~W}=\mathrm{MB}\left(\cos \theta_{1}-\cos \theta_{2}\right) \\
=\mathrm{MB}\left(\cos 0^{\circ}-\cos 45^{\circ}\right)
\end{array} \\
=\mathrm{MB}\left(1-\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)=\left(\frac{\sqrt{2}-1}{\sqrt{2}}\right) \mathrm{MB}
\end{array}$
Also torque acting on the magnet
$\begin{array}{l}
\tau=\mathrm{MB} \sin 45^{\circ}=\frac{\mathrm{MB}}{\sqrt{2}} \\
\mathrm{~W}=(\sqrt{2}-1) \cdot \tau \Rightarrow \tau=\frac{\mathrm{W}}{(\sqrt{2}-1)}
\end{array}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
